title: 'Shazam!' and the Future of the DCEU With David F. Sandberg | Interview
format: Miles Surrey stops by to break down Shazam! and where it leaves the DCEU, then Sean sits down with director David Sandberg on jumping from a viral YouTube short to Annabelle: Creation to superhero previs with action figures.

segments.heading: Shazam! and the DCEU's new approach

films.title: Shazam!
films.note: Miles Surrey's read: an Amblin-style, wish-fulfillment coming-of-age story for its first half (kid discovers powers, buys beer, tries to become a YouTube star), before the back half settles into the standard CG-monster fight with Mark Strong's seven deadly sins.

films.title: Wonder Woman
films.note: The comparison point for tonal missteps: charming and well made right up until it also turns into a fight against a demon monster, played by David Thewlis.

films.title: Aquaman
films.note: "Operatically goofy," and mostly table-setting for a sequel and a Trench spinoff that never quite arrived.

films.title: Justice League
films.note: The DCEU's cautionary tale: a rushed, interconnected tentpole that bombed critically and financially and pushed the studio toward standalone stories instead.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Coming that October, tonally standalone from Shazam! and made for a fraction of the budget, which lowers the risk if it doesn't land.

films.title: Birds of Prey
films.note: Cathy Yan's five-superhero team-up built around Margot Robbie's Harley Quinn, visually nothing like Shazam! or Joker.

films.title: Lights Out
films.note: The two-and-a-half-minute, no-budget short he and his wife Lotta made in Gothenburg went viral on YouTube and got him signed within a day; it became the basis for his feature debut.

films.title: Annabelle: Creation
films.note: His actual first studio film, with about triple the budget of Lights Out. He softened Annabelle's doll design mid-production because it read as too obviously evil for a kid to bring home.

films.title: Shazam!
films.note: He inherited a script that was originally "Shazam versus Black Adam" and helped pare it down; previs for the big effects sequences was often just Sandberg filming himself acting out scenes with action figures.

films.title: Dragged Across Concrete
films.note: His pick for the last great thing he'd seen: "very long and slow, but I really liked it. And it was so brutal."

referenced.group: Also part of the DCEU conversation
films: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
